title: The Triumph of 'The Odyssey,' and the Christopher Nolan Movie Rankings
format: Chris Ryan joins Sean and Amanda to mark 'The Odyssey' opening to a $264M worldwide weekend, then the three rank all thirteen Christopher Nolan features into one negotiated list. Opens with a movie-news block: the Avengers: Doomsday trailer, the paused Paramount-Warner merger, and a festival slate rundown.

films.title: Avengers: Doomsday
films.note: The trailer drops the Monday after 'The Odyssey' opens, timed to Comic-Con. Chris and Sean saw it four months earlier at CinemaCon and found the room's enthusiasm manufactured; the group reads Marvel as 'completely cooked,' with Robert Downey Jr.'s Dr. Doom the one intriguing swing. Still dated against Dune: Part Three.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Held up as the model Doomsday can't match: a decade of buildup paying off in shock and awe. 'I don't know if Namor in distress is gonna do the same thing.'

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The other half of the payoff Marvel earned once and is trying to reverse-engineer with a Secret Wars two-parter.

films.title: The Fantastic Four: First Steps
films.note: Sean 'knew it was over' in the theater; the film vanished after a week and took its characters' stakes for Doomsday with it. Julia Garner's Silver Surfer went undefended.

films.title: Cliff Booth
films.note: Leads a Netflix slate Sean concedes looks strong this year, part of the festival talk.

films.title: Ray Gun
films.note: Brad Bird's long-in-the-making animated film.

films.title: La Bola Negra
films.note: Seen at Cannes; a big awards contender this year.

films.title: Possible Love
films.note: Lee Chang-dong; sounds Venice-bound.

films.title: Animals
films.note: Ben Affleck directs and plays a mayor in a kidnapping story; Kerry Washington co-stars.

films.title: Sacrifice
films.note: Roman Gavras; picked up at TIFF last year.

films.title: Saturn Return
films.note: Greg Kwedar's follow-up to 'Sing Sing.'

films.title: Here Comes the Flood
films.note: The Robert Pattinson / Denzel Washington movie.

films.title: Good Sex
films.note: Lena Dunham.

films.title: The Mosquito Bowl
films.note: Peter Berg's WWII football sports drama.

films.title: I Play Rocky
films.note: World premiering at TIFF.

films.title: Misty Green
films.note: Chris Rock's new film as director; an A24 title.

films.title: Bad Lieutenant Tokyo
films.note: Takashi Miike, world premiering at TIFF.

films.title: Babies
films.note: Lauren Miller Rogen's; the cast includes Seth Rogen.

films.title: A Talent for Murder
films.note: Anton Corbijn, from the play 'Switzerland' about Patricia Highsmith; stars Helen Mirren.

films.title: Behemoth
films.note: Pedro Pascal; Chris expects a Telluride ride.

films.title: A Place in Hell
films.note: Chloe Domont's Christmas movie with Michelle Williams and Daisy Edgar-Jones.

films.title: Jason Bourne
films.note: 'The final Jason Bourne movie was quite poor,' the context for the new Matt Damon / Edward Berger Bourne, reported as one more entry rather than a reboot, with Zendaya rumored to inherit the franchise.

films.title: The Bourne Identity
films.note: Amanda's favorite: the country house and the haircutting. The group agrees Berger is about the best possible choice to continue it.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: A $264.1M worldwide opening ($125M domestic): the biggest debut since 'The Dark Knight Rises' and the year's third-largest, behind only 'Toy Story 5' and the Mario Galaxy movie, for a three-hour R-rated film. The three read it as the skeleton key to Nolan, the movie that makes the whole filmography cohere. Chris's takeaways: the sea-people invention as a xenophobia commentary (Xenia, Zeus's law of the stranger); Odysseus as a bastard given regret Homer never grants him; the siren-song description as some of the best writing Nolan's put on screen; the Hades sequence and Ludwig Goransson's atonal score as career-best craft. Dissents: the John Wick-ish suitor massacre plays flat and Batman-y, and Nolan still gestures at violence he won't show. The revisionist sacking of Troy lands the theme through action rather than voiceover.

segments.heading: The Christopher Nolan Movie Rankings
segments.note: one negotiated list, best to worst
segments.subject: Christopher Nolan

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: No. 1: the compromise winner and Chris's personal favorite. Showing over telling; a third of it silent. Amanda finds the structure a touch too cute; the others give it the top slot to keep 'The Odyssey' off it.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: No. 2. Sean would have it at one; the last two hours are transcendent, the Tom Conti / Casey Affleck scenes among the sickest work Nolan's done, even if the endgame keeps going.

films.title: The Odyssey
films.note: No. 3. Everyone agrees No. 1 would be recency-bias cringe; Amanda privately has it first. Could climb in six months.

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: No. 4. Old tools applied to a new kind of blockbuster. Chris keeps stacking films above it as time goes on; not in his top four.

films.title: Inception
films.note: No. 5. Parked here because most people expect it there and both Sean and Amanda rate it over Memento; Chris would have it at nine.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: No. 6. 'What people like.' Sean's actual No. 1; the group won't risk the backlash of ranking it higher.

films.title: Interstellar
films.note: No. 7. Some of the sickest craft you'll see, but the McConaughey sentimentality tips it over. 'The Interstellar boys are gonna cry.'

films.title: Memento
films.note: No. 8. Best of the Jonathan-scripted films; a 1940s Warner Bros. crime movie in reverse.

films.title: The Prestige
films.note: No. 9. A cult favorite the group appreciates from afar (Hugh Jackman, magic, Bowie as Tesla) but won't rate over Inception.

films.title: The Dark Knight Rises
films.note: No. 10. 'Goes hard when it goes hard,' but often quite silly and plot-incoherent. A comfortable placeholder that spares anyone's feelings.

films.title: Batman Begins
films.note: No. 11. No real villain and a well-worn Bruce Wayne origin; the Scarecrow still terrifies Amanda. Sean would rather rewatch Insomnia.

films.title: Insomnia
films.note: No. 12. The least Nolan-y of the bunch, a studio job he didn't write, but the action's solid and Robin Williams is very good.

films.title: Following
films.note: No. 13 (or 12). The micro-budget debut where everything's already present: the fractured time, the withheld information, a man named Cobb, a Batman sticker on a door.
